{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |French Defence: Advance, Euwe variation| |rd| | |qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd| |bd| |pd|pd|pd|= | | |nd| |pd| | | |= | | |pd|pd|pl| | | |= | | | |pl| | | | |= | | |pl| | |nl| | |= |pl|pl| | | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl| |rl|= || }} =French Defence: Advance Variation, Euwe Variation= With 5...Bd7, Black enters the '''Euwe Variation'''.[http://www.365chess.com/opening.php?m=11&n=1250&ms=e4.e6.d4.d5.e5.c5.c3.Nc6.Nf3.Bd7&ns=3.16.12.17.453.525.454.526.711.1250] This move is less committal than the main line [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e6/2. d4/2...d5/3. e5/3...c5/4. c3/4...Nc6/5. Nf3/5...Qb6|5...Qb6]], as it allows Black to reserve the option of where to place their Queen. In some lines, Black may prefer an eventual ...Qc7, adding pressure to e5. This move also prepares Black's thematically weak light-square Bishop for a possible escape to b5 or a4. =Theory Table= {{ChessTable}}. '''1.e4 e6 2.d4 d5 3.e5 c5 4. c3 Nc6 5. Nf3 Bd7'''
